finding: Environmental stressors trigger measurable shifts in baseline psychological dispositions. Multi-dimensional trait shifts correlate with duration of stressor exposure, with trajectories varying by demographic group; some shifts reverse when stressor is removed while others persist.

Motor system simultaneously encodes multiple observed agents' actions. Identical actions from different agents activate shared motor representation more strongly than single action. Different actions from multiple agents produce opposing effects that cancel in motor representation.

Published effect sizes in a research domain with large reported effect counts exhibit non-random clustering near statistical significance threshold, indicating selective reporting bias obscures true effect size. Multiple independent bias-detection methods converge on presence of publication and p-hacking artifacts.

Cognitive inference abilities decline with age while affective capacities remain stable or improve. Age-related decline in metacognitive calibration is specific to non-social reasoning, sparing social metacognition. Emotional empathy unaffected by age; compassion increases.

Observer's own age biases perception of ambiguous face stimulus. Younger observers perceive image as younger; older observers perceive as older. Effect operates at preconscious level despite brief stimulus exposure and participant unawareness of age-related hypothesis.
